subjectLine: Senolytics mapped to sarcopenia: new mechanism guide 💊
borisTake: This is the mechanistic scaffold I've been waiting for before scaling up any senolytic protocol specifically for muscle preservation — satellite cells aren't depleted, they're being silenced by the senescent niche around them, and that changes the target entirely. If you're doing D+Q cycles and not thinking about skeletal muscle compartment specifically, this review should reorient the protocol.
leadAnalysis: The review maps exactly which cell types accumulate senescence in aged skeletal muscle — satellite cells, fibro-adipogenic progenitors, endothelial cells, immune cells, and even post-mitotic myofibers — and shows how their SASP disrupts the regenerative niche rather than depleting stem cell numbers. The key mechanistic insight is that muscle stem cells in old tissue retain full function when moved to a young environment, meaning sarcopenia is a niche problem, not a stem cell problem. Therapeutically, the authors position senolytics (dasatinib+quercetin, fisetin) and senomorphics (rapamycin at intermittent low dose) as the most mechanistically justified interventions, with intermittent dosing flagged specifically to avoid immunosuppression. Clinical relevance is high: this gives practitioners a clear rationale for senolytic cycling to target muscle aging separately from general healthspan protocols.
oneNumber: 28.8% | Share of adults with at least one 'hassler' in close social network — enough to measurably accelerate epigenetic aging, per PNAS study of 2,345 participants

papers.title: Cellular Senescence in Skeletal Muscle Aging
papers.summary: Comprehensive review mapping senescent cell accumulation across skeletal muscle cell types and positioning senolytics/senomorphics — including D+Q and rapamycin — as mechanistically justified interventions for sarcopenia. The key finding: satellite cells aren't depleted, they're silenced by the senescent niche.

papers.title: Two Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids Show Senolytic Activity In Vitro and In Vivo
papers.summary: α-Eleostearic acid (α-ESA) and its methyl ester demonstrate senolytic activity in cell cultures and mouse models. The structural features driving activity — conjugated double bond configuration and esterification — point toward a new class of dietary-derived senolytics that may carry fewer side effects than current clinical candidates.

papers.title: MG53 in Early Skeletal Muscle Stem Cell Activation: Implications for Aged Muscle Regeneration
papers.summary: Review proposes MG53 (TRIM72), a muscle-enriched E3 ubiquitin ligase, as a stress-responsive regulator stabilising early satellite cell activation in aged muscle. The hypothesis: MG53 mitigates oxidative stress and membrane disruption during the rate-limiting early activation window, which becomes increasingly fragile with age.

papers.title: Therapeutic Targeting of Immune Aging: From Rapamycin to Thymic Rejuvenation
papers.summary: Systematic review of immune aging interventions covering mTOR inhibition (rapamycin/rapalogs), p38 MAPK inhibitors, MSC therapies, and thymic rejuvenation strategies (FOXN1, FGF21, KGF). Intermittent low-dose rapamycin is highlighted as the most evidence-backed approach, with thymic reconstitution positioned as the next frontier.

papers.title: Negative Social Interactions Are Associated With Accelerated Biological Aging
papers.summary: Among 2,345 adults (ages 18–103), having 'hasslers' in close social networks — people who create problems or stress — was associated with measurably faster epigenetic aging. 28.8% of participants had at least one such contact; 10% had two or more. Effect was not confined to marital conflict, extending to all close relationships.

papers.title: Meat Consumption May Protect Cognitive Function in APOE4 Carriers
papers.summary: Karolinska Institutet study in 2,000+ older adults found a negative association between unprocessed meat consumption and cognitive decline specifically in APOE ε4 carriers. The effect may reflect evolutionary alignment — ε4 is the ancestral allele, emerging during a hypercarnivorous phase — and suggests APOE genotyping should inform dietary recommendations rather than one-size-fits-all guidance.

papers.title: BioAge Labs 2026 Pipeline Update
papers.summary: BioAge Labs provided a business update on its clinical-stage pipeline targeting metabolic disease through the biology of aging. The company continues to advance candidates that target aging biology — including compounds identified via its proprietary human aging cohort — as the clinical-stage longevity therapeutics sector matures.
